remained completely well in interval phase

plan to return for removal of ductal stones

hospitalized for pain control with narcotics
fatty atrophy of the pancreatic head
fatty atrophy of the uncinate process
pancreatic duct filled with large calcifications
possible obstruction at the level of the neck
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
activated recombinant human protein C administered
no obvious obstructing calculi in the common bile duct
bedside emergency endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) performed
major papilla of Vater expelling frank pus

cholangiogram normal biliary tree without stones
pancreatogram marked dilatation of the main pancreatic duct
approximately 20 ml of pus evacuated during duct manipulation
pancreatic duct stent placed (5 cm, 5 F)
no more pus draining after stent placement
acute suppuration of the pancreatic duct (ASPD)
contrast-enhanced CT scan of the abdomen performed
inflammatory changes within peripancreatic fat (body and tail) consistent with edema
calculus distal migration towards the sphincter of Oddi
blood cultures positive for Klebsiella ornithinolytica
